Comparison · 2026

contextprompt vs Avoma
for engineering teams

Avoma is a conversation intelligence platform built around sales calls. If your meetings produce code, there's a tool built specifically for that.

Try contextprompt free Free plan · No credit card required
TL;DR

Avoma is a strong conversation intelligence platform with AI scorecards, deal risk signals, and deep CRM sync — purpose-built for sales and revenue teams. contextprompt is purpose-built for engineering teams: it scans your connected repos, maps every coding decision from the transcript to real file paths, and outputs structured tasks ready for Claude Code, Cursor, or any AI coding assistant. If your team ships software, contextprompt wins every sprint planning meeting.

Feature comparison

What Avoma and contextprompt offer for software development teams.

Feature contextprompt Avoma
Meeting transcription ✓ Zoom, Meet, Slack, Teams ✓ All major platforms
AI meeting summary ✓ Included ✓ Included
Speaker identification ✓ With attribution ✓ Included
Repo / codebase scanning ✓ File paths + exports ✗ Not available
Tasks with real file paths ✓ Mapped to your code ✗ Not available
Implementation steps per task ✓ Step-by-step ✗ Not available
AI coding assistant output ✓ Paste into Claude Code ✗ Not available
Task confidence levels ✓ High / Med / Low ✗ Not available
GitHub issue analysis ✓ Pro feature ✗ Not available
AI scorecards – Not applicable ✓ Sales-focused
Deal risk tracking – Not applicable ✓ Revenue intelligence
CRM sync – Not applicable ✓ Salesforce, HubSpot
Free tier 1 hr recording / month 14-day free trial only
Pro pricing $30 / mo (whole account) ~$19 / user / mo (annual)
Developer-focused output ✓ Built for engineers Built for sales teams

Where contextprompt pulls ahead

Avoma optimizes for closing deals. contextprompt optimizes for shipping code.

📁

Meetings mapped to your repo

contextprompt scans your connected repositories before analyzing the transcript. When your team says "update the auth handler" or "fix the date picker component," contextprompt finds src/middleware/auth.ts and src/components/DatePicker.tsx automatically.

🤖

Output designed for AI coding agents

Every extracted task ships with file paths, implementation steps, and the exact transcript quote that triggered it. Paste into Claude Code or Cursor and start implementing immediately. Avoma gives you call summaries — you still have to figure out what to build and where.

💰

Per-account pricing saves real money

contextprompt charges $30/month for the whole account. Avoma charges per seat — a team of five at $19/user/month is $95+/month. For most engineering teams, contextprompt is significantly cheaper while delivering more developer-relevant output.

📊

Avoma wins: sales call intelligence

Avoma's AI scorecards, deal risk tracking, and phrase detection (objections, churn signals) are genuinely powerful for revenue teams. If your team runs sales calls, Avoma is a better fit than contextprompt for that use case.

🔗

Avoma wins: CRM integrations

Avoma has deep two-way sync with Salesforce, HubSpot, and Pipedrive. If your workflow ends with updating a CRM record, Avoma's native integrations handle that automatically. contextprompt doesn't integrate with CRMs.

🚀

contextprompt wins: zero-friction start

Paste a meeting URL, the bot joins, tasks appear. No API key setup, no browser extension, no audio driver configuration. Avoma has no permanent free tier — contextprompt offers 1 hour/month free with no credit card required, so you can evaluate it on real meetings before committing.

Who should pick which

The right tool depends entirely on what your team produces after a meeting ends.

Choose contextprompt if you…

You ship software

  • Sprint planning or architecture meetings → coding tasks
  • Use Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, or similar tools
  • Want tasks that reference real files, not vague descriptions
  • Run engineering standups, design reviews, or retros
  • Have a team of 2–20 engineers (per-account pricing scales well)
  • Want a permanent free tier to evaluate before paying
Choose Avoma if you…

You close deals

  • Primary meeting type is sales calls or customer success
  • Need AI scorecards to coach reps on call performance
  • Want deal risk signals and revenue intelligence
  • Require deep CRM sync (Salesforce, HubSpot, Pipedrive)
  • Run a primarily non-engineering team

Common questions

Answers to what engineering teams ask when comparing these tools.

Is Avoma good for engineering teams?
Avoma is primarily built for sales and revenue teams. Its headline features — AI scorecards, deal risk tracking, and CRM sync — are designed for sales workflows. Engineering teams can use it for transcription and summaries, but it won't produce coding tasks with file paths or implementation steps.
How does contextprompt pricing compare to Avoma for a small team?
contextprompt Pro is $30/month per account, regardless of team size. Avoma starts at approximately $19/user/month billed annually. A team of two pays about $38/month on Avoma; a team of five pays around $95/month. contextprompt stays at $30/month for any team size.
Does contextprompt have AI scorecards like Avoma?
No — scorecards are a sales-oriented feature for evaluating call performance. contextprompt instead rates each extracted coding task high, medium, or low confidence based on how clearly it was discussed and how well it maps to a file in your connected repositories.
What meeting platforms does contextprompt support?
contextprompt sends a bot to any meeting URL — Zoom, Google Meet, Microsoft Teams, and any other platform that supports bot participants. You paste the meeting link into the contextprompt dashboard; the bot joins automatically with no browser extension or desktop app required.
Can I use contextprompt if my team also uses Avoma for sales calls?
Yes. They serve completely different audiences. Your sales team can use Avoma for customer calls and deal tracking while your engineering team uses contextprompt for sprint planning, standups, and architecture meetings. They share no data or configuration.

Engineering meetings should produce tasks, not summaries.

contextprompt joins your call, scans your repos, and extracts structured coding tasks with real file paths — ready for Claude Code. Free plan, no credit card.
